edges.description: EWSR1-WT1 drives expression of epithelial, mesenchymal, and neural markers

nodes.description: The t(11;22)(p13;q12) translocation fuses EWSR1 on chromosome 22 with WT1 on chromosome 11. The resulting fusion protein contains the potent transcriptional activation domain of EWSR1 fused to the zinc finger DNA-binding domain of WT1. This chimeric transcription factor activates aberrant gene expression programs including PDGFA.

biological_processes: positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.reference_title: [Cystic desmoplastic small round cell tumor: tumor development from cystic-"mesothelioblastic" areas?].
evidence.snippet: Using microdissection techniques, EWS-WT1-gene fusion transcripts were detected in the cystic (single-cell-layered), the papillary and the solid tumor proliferations (exon 7 of EWS on chromosome 22 with exon 8 of WT1 on chromosome 11).

nodes.description: DSRCT displays a unique multiphenotypic immunophenotype with co-expression of epithelial (cytokeratin, EMA), mesenchymal (vimentin, desmin), and neural (NSE) markers. This reflects the aberrant differentiation program driven by EWSR1-WT1.
EWSR1-WT1 directly activates transcription of PDGFA, creating an autocrine growth loop that stimulates tumor cell proliferation and may contribute to the characteristic desmoplastic stromal reaction.
